title: Ottelia cygnorum
intro: Ottelia cygnorum, also known as the water wisteria or duck potato, is a perennial aquatic plant native to tropical Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ottelia cygnorum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as dried powder or capsule preparations, to take advantage of its potential health benefits.
taxonomy: Ottelia cygnorum is classified within the family Hydrocharitaceae, which is a group of aquatic plants commonly known as the waterworts. This family is part of the larger order Alismatales, a grouping of flowering plants that are primarily found in aquatic environments. The order Alismatales is further classified under the class Liliopsida, which includes a wide variety of monocots. The class Liliopsida is part of the larger division Magnoliophyta, also known as the flowering plants. This division is further grouped under the kingdom Plantae, which includes all types of vascular plants.
